text:
ANATOMIC PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
DIVISION OF SURGICAL PATHOLOGY / THORACIC PATHOLOGY

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT: Yuen, Margaret
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]          AGE: 64 years          SEX: Female
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Attending Thoracic Oncologist]
PERFORMING RADIOLOGIST (Procedure): [Interventional Radiology Attending]
CLINICAL INDICATION: Known EGFR L858R-mutant pulmonary adenocarcinoma, Stage IIIB at diagnosis, now with disease progression on maintenance pem/Avastin. Repeat CT-guided core needle biopsy of enlarging right upper lobe (RUL) mass performed for clinical trial enrollment and molecular resistance profiling.
SPECIMEN ID: SP-[REDACTED]-A through SP-[REDACTED]-C
SPECIMEN LABEL(S):
  A – Right upper lobe mass, CT-guided core needle biopsy (18-gauge, 4 cores)
  B – Right upper lobe mass, CT-guided core needle biopsy, additional pass for cell block
  C – Right upper lobe mass, CT-guided touch preparation (air-dried and alcohol-fixed)
PROCEDURE DATE: [DATE REDACTED -- TO BE FILLED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
ACCESSION NUMBER: [REDACTED]
SIGNED OUT BY: [Staff Pathologist, Thoracic Pathology]
RESIDENT/FELLOW: [Pathology Fellow]
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


CLINICAL HISTORY (SUPPLIED BY ORDERING SERVICE):
Ms. Yuen is a 64-year-old woman with a history of Stage IIIB (T3N2M0) pulmonary adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe, originally diagnosed at age 62. The primary tumor harbors an EGFR exon 21 L858R sensitizing mutation. She was initially treated with Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily with partial response, followed by disease progression at 14 months with development of systemic and intracranial metastases. Subsequent second-line treatment with Paraplatin/Alimta/Avastin was administered (4 induction cycles followed by maintenance Alimta/bev). She has now developed radiographic disease progression on maintenance therapy, including enlargement of the right upper lobe primary mass to 3.8 cm. Repeat biopsy is requested for clinical trial enrollment (Phase I/II EGFR/MET bispecific antibody study) and comprehensive genomic profiling to characterize acquired resistance mechanisms. Prior tissue biopsy (initial diagnosis): acinar-predominant pulmonary adenocarcinoma, TTF-1 positive. Prior liquid biopsy (Guardant360): EGFR L858R/T790M co-mutation; no MET amplification detected at that time.


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SPECIMEN A and B — RIGHT UPPER LOBE MASS, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

INVASIVE PULMONARY ADENOCARCINOMA, ACINAR-PREDOMINANT PATTERN.

  • No small cell transformation identified.
  • No squamous cell differentiation (no squamous morules, no keratinization, no intercellular bridges).
  • No neuroendocrine morphology (no organoid nesting, no nuclear molding, no salt-and-pepper chromatin suggesting SCLC transformation).
  • Tumor cells demonstrate moderate nuclear enlargement with prominent nucleoli; background desmoplastic stroma present.
  • Focal necrosis identified (approximately 10-15% of tumor area).
  • Lymphovascular invasion: present (focal).
  • Tumor cellularity estimated at approximately 65-70% of submitted tissue.

SPECIMEN C — TOUCH PREPARATION:
  Adequate for evaluation. Malignant epithelial cells with features consistent with adenocarcinoma. No morphologic features to suggest high-grade neuroendocrine transformation.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANCILLARY STUDIES
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Specimen A, FFPE block, 4-micron sections):

  TTF-1 (SP141 clone):              POSITIVE — strong, diffuse nuclear staining in >90% of tumor cells
  Napsin-A:                         POSITIVE — moderate to strong granular cytoplasmic staining
  CK7 (MNF116):                     POSITIVE — diffuse membranous/cytoplasmic staining
  CK20:                             NEGATIVE — supports primary pulmonary rather than gastrointestinal origin
  p40 (BC28):                       NEGATIVE — excludes squamous cell carcinoma
  p63:                              NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin:                    NEGATIVE — no neuroendocrine differentiation
  Chromogranin A:                   NEGATIVE
  CD56 (NCAM):                      NEGATIVE — excludes small cell or large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):                    Approximately 35-40% (high proliferative index; increased from prior biopsy estimate)

  INTERPRETATION: Immunohistochemical profile is consistent with primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma. Importantly, no evidence of phenotypic transformation to squamous cell carcinoma or small cell/neuroendocrine carcinoma is identified, which can occur as a mechanism of acquired resistance to EGFR-targeted therapy.

PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (22C3 pharmDx assay, Dako/Agilent platform; CLIA-certified):
  Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 28%
  Combined Positive Score (CPS): 36
  Interpretation: PD-L1 TPS 28% (increased from 15% at time of initial diagnosis). This score represents PD-L1 expression in viable tumor cells showing partial or complete membrane staining at any intensity. PD-L1 expression has increased compared to the prior diagnostic biopsy (TPS 15%, initial diagnosis), potentially reflecting clonal evolution, tumor heterogeneity, or prior treatment-related immune microenvironment changes.

ALK (D5F3 CDx assay, Ventana):     NEGATIVE — no ALK protein expression
  (performed for re-confirmation in setting of re-biopsy; consistent with prior negative result)

ROS1 IHC (D4D6 clone):             NEGATIVE
  (reflexed for re-confirmation)

MOLECULAR / NGS TESTING:
  Status: SPECIMEN SUBMITTED FOR COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING — RESULTS PENDING
  Platform: Foundation One CDx (324-gene hybrid capture panel); performed on Specimen A (FFPE block)
  EGFR cobas PCR (rapid reflex): EGFR L858R DETECTED (exon 21) — preliminary rapid PCR result; comprehensive NGS panel results pending (see above).
  Additional rapid molecular testing ordered per clinical trial protocol requirements: MET amplification by FISH, EGFR T790M by cobas — RESULTS PENDING AT TIME OF SIGN-OUT.
  Estimated turnaround: 10-14 business days.
  Specimen adequacy for NGS: ADEQUATE. Estimated tumor nuclei fraction: 65%. DNA yield sufficient for 324-gene panel and additional FISH studies.

FISH STUDIES:
  MET FISH (7q31.2; Vysis MET/CEP7 dual-color probe): — PENDING
  EGFR amplification FISH (7p12; for clinical context): — PENDING
  ALK FISH (2p23.2-p23.1; Vysis LSI ALK break-apart): — PENDING (re-confirmation)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GROSS DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SPECIMEN A — labeled "right upper lobe mass core biopsy": Received in 10% neutral buffered formalin are four tan-white, soft to focally firm cylindrical core needle biopsy tissue fragments measuring 0.8 cm, 0.9 cm, 1.0 cm, and 1.1 cm in greatest length x 0.1 cm diameter each. One core demonstrates a grossly tan-yellow, focally hemorrhagic appearance. All four cores are submitted entirely in one cassette (A1), with one core reserved for potential additional molecular studies.

SPECIMEN B — labeled "right upper lobe mass additional pass / cell block": Received in 10% neutral buffered formalin is a 0.4 cm x 0.3 cm x 0.2 cm tan-white fragment of soft tissue. Entirely submitted in one cassette (B1) for cell block preparation. Cell block shows adequate tumor cellularity (estimated 60-65% tumor nuclei).

SPECIMEN C — labeled "RUL touch prep, air-dried" and "RUL touch prep, 95% ethanol-fixed": Two sets of glass slides received in slide carrier, prepared at time of procedure. Diff-Quik-stained (air-dried) and Papanicolaou-stained (ethanol-fixed) preparations reviewed.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Hematoxylin and eosin-stained sections from Specimens A and B are examined. The biopsy cores show an infiltrating neoplasm composed predominantly of gland-forming epithelial cells arranged in an acinar pattern. The neoplastic glands are irregular in size and shape, lined by cuboidal to columnar cells with moderate to markedly enlarged nuclei, vesicular chromatin, and often conspicuous central nucleoli. The cytoplasm is moderate in amount, pale eosinophilic, and focally mucinous. Occasional nuclear pseudoinclusions are noted, a characteristic feature of pulmonary adenocarcinoma. The tumor glands are embedded within a desmoplastic fibrous stroma with a chronic inflammatory infiltrate. A minor component of solid growth pattern is noted (estimated <10%), not sufficient to alter the acinar-predominant classification per IASLC/ATS/ERS 2021 criteria. Focal central coagulative necrosis is present within some glandular lumina.

No organoid nesting, trabecular architecture, nuclear molding, granular "salt-and-pepper" chromatin, or Indian file growth pattern is identified to suggest neuroendocrine differentiation. The neoplastic cells do not demonstrate intercellular bridges or overt keratinization. No spindle cell component is identified. No features of sarcomatoid or pleomorphic carcinoma are seen.

The background non-neoplastic lung tissue shows mild chronic inflammatory changes and focal organizing pneumonia pattern, likely related to prior radiation therapy (the patient received SRS to intracranial sites; however, the right upper lobe was not directly irradiated) or prior chemotherapy effect. Reactive type II pneumocyte hyperplasia is noted at the biopsy edges.

Mitotic figures: approximately 6-8 per 10 high-power fields, consistent with increased proliferative activity as corroborated by Ki-67 index (~35-40%).

Touch preparations (Specimen C): The air-dried and ethanol-fixed preparations show loosely cohesive clusters and single malignant epithelial cells. The cells have eccentric nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and moderate pale cytoplasm, consistent with adenocarcinoma. No necrotic background. No neuroendocrine features.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COMMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

This repeat biopsy of the right upper lobe mass in this patient with a known EGFR L858R-mutant pulmonary adenocarcinoma demonstrates persistent adenocarcinoma of acinar-predominant type without histologic transformation. In the context of EGFR TKI resistance, phenotypic transformation to small cell carcinoma (SCLC) or squamous cell carcinoma represents a well-documented resistance mechanism. This specimen does not demonstrate such transformation, which is consistent with the clinical decision to pursue EGFR/MET-targeted investigational therapy.

Notably, PD-L1 TPS has increased to 28% from the initial 15% measured at diagnosis. This finding may reflect clonal evolution of the tumor immune microenvironment, shifts in the dominant tumor clone under selective treatment pressure, or inter-biopsy sampling heterogeneity. The increased PD-L1 expression could be of potential relevance if checkpoint inhibitor therapy is considered as part of the investigational regimen.

The Ki-67 proliferative index of approximately 35-40% is higher than typically reported for low-grade adenocarcinoma and may reflect increasing tumor aggressiveness in the setting of multi-line therapy and emerging resistance mechanisms.

Comprehensive genomic profiling (Foundation One CDx) and MET FISH results are pending at time of sign-out. Results will be addended to this report upon availability.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST ATTESTATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

I have personally reviewed the submitted slides, gross descriptions, and ancillary study results noted above. The diagnoses rendered are based on morphologic evaluation, immunohistochemical profile, and available clinical context. I concur with the diagnosis as stated.

[Electronically signed by: Attending Staff Pathologist, Division of Thoracic Pathology]
[Fellow cosign: Pathology Fellow, PGY-5]

Addendum pending: Foundation One CDx comprehensive genomic profiling; MET FISH; EGFR T790M cobas; ALK FISH re-confirmation.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SPECIMEN DISPOSITION AND ADEQUACY SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Specimen Adequacy: ADEQUATE for diagnosis and molecular studies
  Tumor Cellularity (estimated): 65-70%
  FFPE block available for reflex/additional studies: YES (Specimen A, block A1)
  Residual unstained slides cut and available: YES (10 charged slides held for molecular/special stains)
  Excess formalin-fixed tissue: none (tissue exhausted in processing)
  Cell block (Specimen B): ADEQUATE, available for additional IHC if required

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSES CONSIDERED AND EXCLUDED:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1. Small cell lung carcinoma transformation: EXCLUDED — no neuroendocrine morphology; synaptophysin, chromogranin, CD56 all negative; no nuclear molding or geographic necrosis of SCLC-type.
  2. Squamous cell carcinoma (primary or transformed): EXCLUDED — p40 and p63 negative; no keratinization or intercellular bridges; TTF-1 and Napsin-A strongly positive.
  3. Metastatic adenocarcinoma (non-pulmonary primary): EXCLUDED — IHC profile (TTF-1+/Napsin-A+/CK7+/CK20-) strongly supports primary pulmonary origin; clinical history consistent.
  4. Large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma: EXCLUDED — no organoid architecture; neuroendocrine markers negative; no rosette formation.
  5. Pleomorphic/sarcomatoid carcinoma: EXCLUDED — no spindle cell or giant cell component identified.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
END OF REPORT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
